title: ABOUT DARKHORSE EDITIONS
wikitext:
[]: ABOUT DARKHORSE EDITIONS             Quote Post by Jäeger » Tue Dec 09, 2014 1:18 pm Well, I'm pretty tired of Shinji Ikari's Rising Project spanish publisher (2014 and still volume 12) so I pretend to purchase the english release. So, It's the darkhorse version a replica of the Japanese tankobon?? I'm talking about size and if it comes with the usual sleeve for the volume. My doubts come from the Eva comic Antholy edition, because it's bigger than your usual manga and the cover.....no sleeve, just printed on paperback

[]:             Quote Post by vito » Tue Dec 09, 2014 1:26 pm My guess is not. Dustjackets are fairly uncommon in the English market.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Tue Dec 09, 2014 2:46 pm None of my non-Japanese manga have sleeves. My Dark Horse volumes (other than Akira , which is a special case) are pretty much the same size as my Japanese ones, though (about 1mm higher), whereas the Viz ones are distinctly larger.

[]:             Quote Post by Jäeger » Tue Dec 09, 2014 3:04 pm Thank You for wour answers, It seem I'll have to follow the spanish pace PD PD?? Why do they publish such shitty editions??

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Dec 10, 2014 7:04 pm I only have a small collection of various English manga, but none of them have sleeves.

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Wed Dec 10, 2014 7:28 pm None of the English manga I've gotten have ever had sleeves going back nearly 20 years.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Dec 11, 2014 8:23 pm I have four volumes of Blood Alone by Masayuki Takano localized by Infinity Studios that have glossy sleeves. That's about all I'm aware of, although to be fair, I'm not really into manga.

Article: ABOUT DARKHORSE EDITIONS
[]: ABOUT DARKHORSE EDITIONS             Quote Post by Jäeger » Tue Dec 09, 2014 1:18 pm Well, I'm pretty tired of Shinji Ikari's Rising Project spanish publisher (2014 and still volume 12) so I pretend to purchase the english release. So, It's the darkhorse version a replica of the Japanese tankobon?? I'm talking about size and if it comes with the usual sleeve for the volume. My doubts come from the Eva comic Antholy edition, because it's bigger than your usual manga and the cover.....no sleeve, just printed on paperback

[]:             Quote Post by vito » Tue Dec 09, 2014 1:26 pm My guess is not. Dustjackets are fairly uncommon in the English market.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Tue Dec 09, 2014 2:46 pm None of my non-Japanese manga have sleeves. My Dark Horse volumes (other than Akira , which is a special case) are pretty much the same size as my Japanese ones, though (about 1mm higher), whereas the Viz ones are distinctly larger.

[]:             Quote Post by Jäeger » Tue Dec 09, 2014 3:04 pm Thank You for wour answers, It seem I'll have to follow the spanish pace PD PD?? Why do they publish such shitty editions??

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Dec 10, 2014 7:04 pm I only have a small collection of various English manga, but none of them have sleeves.

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Wed Dec 10, 2014 7:28 pm None of the English manga I've gotten have ever had sleeves going back nearly 20 years.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Dec 11, 2014 8:23 pm I have four volumes of Blood Alone by Masayuki Takano localized by Infinity Studios that have glossy sleeves. That's about all I'm aware of, although to be fair, I'm not really into manga.
